content.seo_title: Freelancers: Passive Income to Scale AI SaaS (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Break the freelance income ceiling. Use this intermediate checklist to build scalable passive income assets using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To escape the trade-time-for-money trap, you must productize your expertise into micro-SaaS assets. By leveraging AI-driven workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate feature deployment and maintenance, turning one-off client deliverables into recurring, low-touch revenue streams that scale without adding hours to your work week.
content.intro:
The freelance treadmill is a brutal cycle: you land a high-ticket contract, you ship, you get paid, and then you are back to zero, hunting for the next lead. Your capacity is locked behind the clock, and your income is tethered to your physical presence in the IDE. It is a formula for burnout, not wealth.

But the architectural shift is here. By treating your code as an asset rather than a delivery, you can pivot from service provider to product owner. Using a stack of Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous iteration, you can build, deploy, and maintain scalable micro-SaaS products that work while you sleep.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Find a repetitive task you've solved for three different clients and turn that logic into a standalone tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the common pain points in my project history and suggest 3 high-demand, low-complexity micro-SaaS ideas that can be built as a subscription-based API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'analyze project directory for common code patterns and suggest productizable modules'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready, modular architecture that allows for rapid feature injection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable SaaS boilerplate using a serverless backend and a clean UI component library, optimized for rapid feature expansion.

steps.title: Automate Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Offload the boilerplate coding to Claude Code so you can focus on high-level system design and business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that handles user authentication and subscription gating using Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement stripe checkout and middleware for subscription-based route protection'

steps.title: Implement Continuous Deployment
steps.description: Ensure your product is always live and reachable by automating the CI/CD pipeline, reducing manual overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests and deploys to Vercel/AWS on every main branch merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup automated testing and deployment pipeline for production environment'

steps.title: Productize the Maintenance Cycle
steps.description: Use AI agents to monitor logs and performance, making your micro-SaaS truly 'passive' by catching bugs before users do.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a monitoring script that pings the health check endpoint and alerts me via Slack if latency exceeds 500ms.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add automated uptime monitoring and slack notification integration'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'boring' problems; high-demand micro-SaaS products often solve unsexy B2B workflow gaps.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first; it forces a cleaner API design and saves you from debugging production fires later.
Never build a custom dashboard if a third-party API or SaaS tool can handle the data visualization for you.
Focus on a single 'killer feature' that provides immediate value; avoid feature creep that kills maintenance efficiency.
Keep your infrastructure serverless to eliminate the need for manual server patching or scaling operations.
resources: Stripe API Docs for SaaS: https://stripe.com/docs/billing
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on building a simple landing page to validate demand before adding complex features. Iterate based on actual user feedback, not your assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop chasing clients and start building assets that pay you indefinitely. The tools are ready, the workflow is defined, and the market is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
